name: Aidos
description: Aidos is the Greek goddess of shame, reverence, and respect. She embodies the feeling of humility and the sense of right and wrong that prevents humans from committing dishonorable acts. Aidos is closely linked to Nemesis, the goddess of retribution, as both goddesses work to maintain balance and order in the world. Aidos is not typically given extensive myths or stories of her own, but she is frequently invoked as the personification of moral conscience and the fear of social disapproval. She represents the internal restraint that keeps individuals from violating societal norms and ethical principles. Her influence is seen in the concepts of piety, modesty, and the avoidance of hubris. She is often depicted as a young woman, veiled or with downcast eyes, symbolizing her association with shame and humility. Aidos is considered a guardian of social order and a protector of the vulnerable, particularly suppliants and the poor. Her presence is a reminder of the importance of respecting boundaries and maintaining a sense of honor. She stands in opposition to shamelessness and the disregard for moral codes, representing the internal compass that guides individuals towards ethical behavior and prevents them from straying into immoral or disrespectful actions. The loss of Aidos from the world would signify the collapse of morality and the descent into utter lawlessness and disregard for social harmony.
subtitle: Goddess of Shame, Reverence, and Respect

keyMyths.title: The Departure of Aidos
keyMyths.description: As humanity descended into wickedness and lost all sense of shame, Aidos was said to have departed the earth, abandoning mortals to their own depravity. This departure signified the complete breakdown of moral order and the triumph of hubris.

keyMyths.title: Aidos and Suppliants
keyMyths.description: Aidos was considered a protector of suppliants, those who sought refuge and pleaded for mercy. To violate the rights of a suppliant was to offend Aidos and invite her wrath, along with the punishment of Nemesis.

keyMyths.title: Aidos and Hubris
keyMyths.description: Aidos stands in direct opposition to hubris, excessive pride and arrogance that leads to downfall. Those who succumb to hubris disregard Aidos and ultimately face retribution for their lack of respect and humility.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: A didactic poem offering insights into Greek morality and the importance of respect.
sources.text: A didactic poem offering insights into Greek morality and the importance of respect.

sources.author: Homer
